publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 13-16. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: My dearest Cock. Our dear Hen seems to me much better today, & says she is so herself. Her little nasty cough is not so bad.-- I am delightfully well myself, & in all aspects like a dear & good little Bird.-- I have not yet succeeded in getting any horse. Joseph has been looking out for one, but

doubts if he shall find what he thinks suitable.-- He seems so exceedingly cautious about it, that I cannot help thinking he suspects there is good reason to be so. At all events, I am not inclined to be otherwise than very cautious myself.-- The Hen thought the Bird should take a drive this afternoon, since it could not ride; so I had a nice pair of post horses to the Britska & Mrs. Henry Siddons & I took a very pretty & pleasant drive together. I have also walked today for about
half an hour, & in short am a dear little bird.--
I have hired a very nice harp, which is just come, & is gone up into my room, where it is to live, since this tiny house has but little spare space.--
I have been lamentably idle today. What else could be expected, the first Hen-day?--
My dearest, I am longing to hear from you tomorrow morning.-- The Hen says she always knows when I am writing to you, by my countenance-- the Cock countenance.-- I think

it is almost worth this little absence, in order to feel more forcibly how very very much I love you. You are indeed a dear good master, & so kind to your little own Bird. It makes the poor little thing very happy to think of its Cock.--
Miss Montgomery was here yesterday when I came, but has left Southampton today. I was glad to see her, & so was she to see the Bird.--
I forgot to tell you that Tubbs is rather in tribulation about your hats. He consulted me yesterday morning before I came away, & so I sat

in judgment on this momentous subject. I decreed that I certainly would not bring one down here for you, as I was sure you would return to Ockham [diagonal line crossed out] before you came, & could get what you wanted for yourself.--
Tubbs says we want a new dining-room tray-stand, the one there is being unsafe.--
The Coachman &c were so exact yesterday morning, that I kept them waiting. But I believe you have not threatened to dismiss
the poor Bird for this crime.--
My dearest husband, the poor little thing wants her master sadly. How she will chirp at him, when he comes!-- I send you now six good chirps.--
Believe me, Your very affecte Birdie, A. Ada King
